title: Senior Software Engineer, £70,000 To £85,000, UK
jd_text: What we're buildingLifted is fixing one of the UK's most broken sectors: care. We've built compliance and immigration tech that helps care providers hire both locally and internationally, while automating the painful Home Office requirements, right-to-work checks, and all the paperwork that usually makes this take months. We're now expanding our product surface area. We're not a typical recruiting platform - we're infrastructure for the entire sector.Why this mattersA lot of engineering roles are pretty removed from their impact. This one isn't, we're fixing critical infrastructure in a sector that's struggling. The systems you build have immediate, tangible impact.The roleWe need senior engineers who can own features end-to-end - from understanding the problem through to shipping the solution.What that looks like:You'll work directly with stakeholders to understand what needs buildingYou'll start with feature goals, and have ownership of implementation details through to roll outYou'll make pragmatic scope decisions when trading off speed vs completenessYou'll ship features that directly impact our usersWhat support looks like:You'll work collaboratively with the team - we're building our documentation practices as we scaleYou'll work with the broader team to align on prioritiesYou're not alone, but trusted to drive things forwardThe stack (high level)Backend: Node.js, PostgreSQL, GraphQL, TypeScriptFrontend: SolidJS, TypeScriptInfrastructure: AWS, TerraformTools: Cursor, Linear, Notion, GitHubPreferred: TypeScript & Node.js experienceBut not mandatory: We care more about good engineering judgment and the ability to ship than specific techNice to have: ML/AI experience - we're expanding our AI capabilitiesWhat we're looking forYou should apply if you:Have built and shipped production systems before (we need senior-level judgment)Have experience at the startup-to-scale transition - managing technical debt, building for scale, and keeping things reliable as they growEnjoy talking to stakeholders and understanding the problem spaceWrite code that other people can understand and maintainHave worked remotely and communicate well asyncAre energised by early-stage environments where things are still being figured outLogisticsSalary: £70,000 to £85,000 depending on experienceLocation: Remote (UK based) or hybrid, we have an office in central LondonHours: Full-time/Contractors
arr_raw: Remote (UK based) or hybrid, we have an office in central London
tech_raw: Node.js, PostgreSQL, GraphQL, TypeScript, SolidJS, TypeScript, AWS, Terraform, Cursor, Linear, Notion, GitHub, TypeScript & Node.js, ML/AI
label: good_fit

title: DevOps Engineer
jd_text:
CloudSys Technologies is a fast-growing cloud services provider that helps businesses scale their operations through innovative solutions. We are searching for a DevOps Engineer to join our fully remote team and help us streamline our deployment processes.

**Responsibilities:**
- Manage and optimize cloud infrastructure using Nodejavascript.
- Collaborate with development teams to enhance CI/CD pipelines.
- Monitor system performance and troubleshoot issues proactively.
- Document processes and share knowledge with team members.

**Requirements:**
- Solid experience with NodeJS.
- Familiarity with containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes).
- Understanding of scripting languages.
- Strong communication skills and a team-oriented mindset.

**Benefits:**
- Salary of £75,000-£95,000.
- Fully remote working environment.
- Flexible working hours.
- Opportunities for professional development and training.

_Note: Familiarity with AI/ML is nice to have but not required._
tech_raw: Nodejavascript, NodeJS, Docker, Kubernetes, scripting languages, AI/ML
label: good_fit

title: DevOps Engineer
jd_text:
TechStream Innovations is a technology-driven company focused on delivering innovative solutions to our clients. We are currently seeking a DevOps Engineer to enhance our development and deployment processes. 

Responsibilities:
- Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines.
- Automate infrastructure and deployment processes using Nodejs.
- Collaborate with development teams to improve system reliability and performance.
- Implement machine learning systems to optimize DevOps workflows.

Requirements:
- Experience in DevOps practices and tools.
- Strong knowledge of Node.js for automation tasks.
- Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP) is a must.
- Nice to have: experience in containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).

Benefits:
- Salary range of £60,000 to £80,000 per annum.
- Hybrid working arrangement with 2-3 days in the office.
- Comprehensive health benefits and retirement plans.
- Ongoing training and certification opportunities.

title: Senior Software Engineer - Web (m/f/d)
jd_text: Company DescriptionFlink is the leading quick commerce player in Europe. We are on a mission to give people back time: You can order your groceries through our app and we deliver to your doorstep in minutes. We carry 2,500 products with a focus on the leading brands in each category. We aim for sustainability by making deliveries on electric bikes and utilizing recyclable packaging.Flink launched in February 2021 and already operates in more than 90 cities in Germany, and the Netherlands. The business generated more than EUR 500m in revenues in 2023 and is operationally profitable. Flink is backed by the world’s most renowned tech investors, including Prosus, Mubadala, and Bond, and strategic investors like Doordash and REWE. We take great pride in being an inclusive employer that offers equal opportunities, and we celebrate the diversity and multiculturalism of our team. We're growing fast and always pushing ourselves to do beJob DescriptionArchitect & Build: Design, develop, and operate resilient, high-performance, and secure web experiences using technologies like Vue.js, Node.js, TypeScript, Nuxt, and NextJS. You will build the features that customers interact with daily, from secure authentication flows to engaging gamification experiences.Lead Complex Feature Delivery: Own the end-to-end delivery of significant features, from technical design through implementation to production monitoring. You will tackle challenging problems like building fraud-resistant systems, creating seamless onboarding experiences, and developing self-service profile management.Champion Web as an Experimentation Platform: Help establish our web platform as the fastest path from idea to validated learning. You will build with A/B testing and rapid iteration in mind, enabling the business to experiment at speed and validate ideas fast.Drive Frontend Excellence: Advocate for and implement best practices in testing, code quality, and frontend architecture within your team. You will lead code reviews, mentor other engineers, and raise the bar for what good looks like.Instrument for Observability: Ensure the features you build are observable and reliable. You will implement monitoring, error tracking, and performance measurement that gives the team confidence in production and enables data-driven improvements.Collaborate Deeply: Work hand-in-hand with Product Managers, Backend Engineers, Designers, and Marketing stakeholders to shape solutions that balance customer needs, technical constraints, and business goals.Contribute to Cross-Team Initiatives: Participate in frontend architecture discussions and contribute to shared tooling and patterns that benefit other teams in the Consumer domain. Your influence will extend beyond your immediate team as you help shape web best practices.Foster Security Awareness: In a team that handles authentication and fraud prevention, security is essential. You will follow security best practices, identify potential vulnerabilities, and help ensure customer data and accounts stay safe.QualificationsSolid experience in frontend software engineering, with a track record of designing, building, and maintaining complex, production-grade web applications. We value quality of impact over years of tenure.Strong proficiency in modern JavaScript/TypeScript and frameworks like Vue.js or React.js.Hands-on experience with modern frontend architecture, including server-side rendering (e.g., Next.js or Nuxt.js) and understanding of how frontend systems scale.Experience building secure, reliable user-facing features, ideally in areas like authentication, user management, or e-commerce.Demonstrated ability to lead feature delivery end-to-end, mentor other engineers, and drive quality improvements within a team.Experience with modern development practices including CI/CD pipelines, automated testing (unit, integration, E2E), and observability for frontend applications.Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners including Product, Design, and Marketing.AI-assisted development experience and enthusiasm, actively leveraging coding agents like Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or similar tools to accelerate your workflow. We believe AI tools are essential for modern development.Nice to HaveExperience building authentication, identity, or security-focused featuresBackground in gamification, loyalty programs, or marketing technologyExperience with fraud prevention or risk systemsFamiliarity with e-commerce, marketplaces, or quick commerceFrontend observability experience (Sentry, Datadog, Lighthouse, Core Web Vitals)Additional InformationA €1000 annual L&D budget as well as individual coaching options to ensure you have plenty of opportunities to learn, grow and achieve your goals26 days of vacation, +1 day every year up to a maximum of 30 daysA mobility budget of 35 EUR per month for Deutschland Ticket subsidyA discount on your Urban Sports Club membershipAttractive company pension optionsUnlimited access to an e-learning and development platform, MyAcademy, including online German coursesOnline discounts with Corporate Benefits and Future BensA cool discount off your personal Flink orders; be the first to test out new products!A modern and dog-friendly office in the heart of Berlin - lots of delicious lunch spots available within short walking distanceIt is our commitment that every applicant will be evaluated according to their skills regardless of age, gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status, or religion.

title: Junior Full Stack PHP Software Engineer
jd_text: We’re looking for a UK-based Junior Full Stack Engineer to support development on our sustainability and digital learning platforms.You’ll be a curious and ambitious engineer who takes pride in writing clean, thoughtful code, enjoys solving real-world problems, and is excited to grow your skills while building technology that makes a meaningful impact.The Role at a Glance:Junior Full Stack EngineerOld Street, London / Hybrid Working / 1 day a week in the office£35,000Plus fantastic benefits: 8% employer pension contribution, mental health support, fitness allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity pay, volunteer days, and more…Permanent - Full TimeProduct / Service: Sustainability Consultancy; Training & EventsCulture: Close knit, small and agile company, flat hierarchy, direct communication with director and hiring managers alike** Heads-Up - We really value a short intro covering note :-) **Who we are:Action Sustainability is a leading consultancy with the aim of inspiring sustainable business.Our team comprises 60+ full and part-time employees and associates. Many of our clients are sector leaders on sustainability and supply chain issues, and we are proud of our reputation for leading the thinking in this area.Action Sustainability drives lasting change in sustainability through innovative technology, expert advisory, and award-winning learning solutions. They partner with leading organisations, including the NHS and Houses of Parliament, to create measurable impact across industries. As the delivery team behind the Supply Chain Sustainability School, we lead global collaboration to accelerate sustainability.An accredited Living Wage employer, we’ve been recognised by Best Companies with a 'One to Watch' status for their commitment to an outstanding workplace. We prioritise regular wellbeing check-ins, ensure employees are heard, and offer mental health support. Our competency framework enables employees to shape their career paths with clear development goals, while managers receive coaching, mentoring, and peer-sharing opportunities to enhance their leadership skills. This comprehensive approach supports an environment where personal and professional growth thrive.About the Role:You’ll work closely with senior engineers to build and improve features that impact thousands of learners, while developing your skills in modern web development and AI-assisted engineering.We make as much use of AI as possible and intend to expand its use so you’ll be encouraged to use AI tools responsibly to learn faster and ship better.Key Responsibilities:• Support the build and maintenance of custom Moodle plugins, themes and features (with guidance)• Write clean, reusable PHP and contribute to modern, responsive UI work (HTML/CSS/JS)• Assist with RESTful APIs and system integrations (implementing defined tasks)• Fix bugs, improve existing code, and contribute to small-to-medium enhancements• Write and maintain unit tests (and contribute to Behat tests where relevant)• Participate in code reviews as a learner (receiving feedback and making improvements)System Support & Quality• Help troubleshoot issues and improve performance under supervision• Follow best practices for accessibility, GDPR and data protection (with support)• Contribute to QA processes and data integrity checksCollaboration• Work within a cross-functional agile team (engineers, product designer, product manager)• Communicate progress clearly and ask for help early when blocked• Engage with stakeholders (e.g., Learning Technologists) mainly via your team leadAbout You:• English proficiency and UK work authorisation• 1–2 years commercial experience (or equivalent demonstrable experience/portfolio) in • • PHP/web developmentWorking Knowledge of:• PHP fundamentals• HTML, CSS3, JavaScript• Relational databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL)• GitDesirable:• Exposure to Moodle or other LMS platforms• Docker basics• Any experience using AI coding tools effectively• Familiarity with WordPressWhat’s on Offer:• A competitive market salary of £35,000• 25 days + 8 statutory holidays, plus an extra day for your birthday• Hybrid working• 8% employer pension contribution• If we exceed targets, the full team gets rewarded through our profit share scheme• Discretionary bonus• Fitness allowance• Enhanced maternity/paternity pay and childcare schemes• Volunteer days• Access to our vibrant Old Street office featuring a lounge, café bar, gym, and rooftop terrace• Leverage professional title: Senior Software Engineer - Backend AI Native
jd_text: Location- Glasgow, HybridSalary - £65,000-80,000About SimpleSimple exists to make personalised healthcare easier to access, understand and act on. Our goal is to remove the friction people face when trying to get the treatments and support they need to feel their best. We’re building a modern, scalable platform from the ground up, powered by AI-native engineering that allows small, senior teams to deliver meaningful impact quickly.This is a unique opportunity to join at the earliest stage and directly shape Simple’s platform, engineering standards and future technical direction.What You’ll Be DoingJoin one of Simple’s first AI-Engineering teams a lean, senior, high-ownership engineering squad. Work in a spec-first, AI-native SDLC: AI generates code, tests, and documentation; you architect, review, integrate, and assure quality. Build core components of Simple’s healthcare platform. Own services end-to-end: design, implementation, observability, reliability, performance. Elevate engineering standards across pods, codify reusable patterns, and help define the golden paths for platform development. Help shape the engineering culture as one of the foundational hires, mentoring, pairing, cross-reviewing and sharing knowledge as we grow. What we’re looking for:EssentialExperience building production-grade software in modern languages (TypeScript/Node.js (Preferred), Python, Java, Go - any strong engineering foundation is fine).Experience building cloud-native systems (AWS preferred): Lambdas, ECS/Fargate, API Gateways, serverless or containerised architectures.Strong skills in automated testing, CI/CD, and modern practices.Comfortable working with generative AI tools (or strong willingness to adopt them) to accelerate development.Deep understanding of software fundamentals: architecture, clean code, performance, observability, maintainability.Strong ownership mindset: you care about outcomes, not just code.Excellent collaborator: you work well in small, high-trust environments and you’re comfortable with pairing, knowledge sharing, and cross-pod communication.DesirableExperience in healthcare, digital services, patient journeys or regulated environments (not required).Full-stack experience: Comfortable working across the stack using React/Next.js when required, though core strengths are backend, cloud-native systems and platform architecture.Experience building scalable platforms, reusable components, or high-leverage engineering systems.Curiosity about AI-native development and excitement about being part of an early shift in engineering.Interview ProcessIntro call - meet Talent & understand your motivations. Technical interview - architecture discussion, pairing, AI-workflow exercise. Final interview - culture, ownership, leadership behaviours Please note that we are unable to offer sponsorship for this position. All applicants must have the right to work in the UK at the time of applicationBasics you can expect:🏖️ 33 days (Inclusive of Bank Holidays) + 1 day after 2 years of service, up to 5 extra days🎂 Extra day off for your birthday💸 Company Share Scheme🏥 Bupa Private Healthcare🏦 Income Protection🚲 Cycle to Work Scheme💪 My Gym Discounts💰 Enhanced Company Pension Scheme🥳 Quarterly Team Training and Social Budget👶 Enhanced Maternity and Paternity Leave💸 Rewarding bonus incentive scheme☎️ 24/7 helpline for physical and mental health support, counselling, and other wellbeing resources.🛍️ Love2Shop Discount🏠 Hybrid workingOffice extras:🍎 Fresh fruit☕ A free coffee machineThe Simple Online Healthcare Promise:Founded in 2015, we started off as a small pharmacy with big dreams. Today, we are trusted by over 3000 patients per day, making sure they receive their medication safely and on time. We're always on the lookout for talent. If you're passionate about helping make lives easier, we'd love to hear from you.Join us on our journey to create affordable health solutions. Apply today to be part of a team that values growth, collaboration, and the simplification of healthcare. Your journey towards creating impactful experiences in the healthcare industry starts here.Ready to make a difference? Apply now!Simple Online Healthcare is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer.
